steps.title: Identify the new independent clause
steps.content: The quotation from Alfred Nobel ends after direction. What follows—In 1952, for instance, judges recognized—is a complete, independent clause (subject: judges; verb: recognized), not a continuation of the quotation.

steps.title: Apply the clause-joining rule
steps.content:
Two independent clauses in the same sentence must be joined by a semicolon or comma + coordinating conjunction.

D uses a semicolon to properly connect the definition with the concrete example.

A adds a misplaced and,, B leaves no punctuation (creating ambiguity), and C uses a comma alone (a comma splice).

This matches choice D — D is correct.
